question_title: Will the interest in “ahmadinejad” change between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-11 according to Google Trends?
summary: Google Trends interest in "ahmadinejad" spiked to 100 on Mar 1 due to reports he was killed in US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran. It has already decayed to 35 by Mar 3 (65% drop in 2 days). With 8 more days until Mar 11, this classic post-spike decay will drive the value well below the "Doesn't change" threshold of 32. The baseline for this term was 0 for 19 consecutive days before the spike. Monte Carlo simulation with generous tail assumptions (ongoing conflict re-spike scenarios) still produces 99.6% probability of Decreases. A modest discount accounts for residual uncertainty from potential unprecedented Ahmadinejad-specific developments in the active Iran conflict.

factors.description: Classic post-spike decay: Value went 100→60→35 in 2 days (40-42% daily decay). Monte Carlo with generous tail assumptions still shows 99.6% Decreases. 8 more days of decay makes Mar 11 value far below 32 threshold.
Person-specific search term decays faster than topic searches. Even with ongoing Iran conflict, users search 'Iran war' not 'ahmadinejad' after initial news cycle passes.
Baseline was 0 for 19 consecutive days before the spike. Natural resting state for this search term is near zero, far below the 32 threshold needed for 'Doesn't change'.
Active US-Israel-Iran conflict ongoing, with Ahmadinejad's alive/dead status still contested. Creates nonzero probability of a secondary search spike if major Ahmadinejad-specific news breaks.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement differences could shift values by 1-3 points, but affects both dates similarly. Marginal impact on directional outcome.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (95%)**

The key dynamics:
- "ahmadinejad" spiked to 100 on Mar 1 due to reports of his death in US-Israeli strikes on Iran, already decaying to 35 by Mar 3
- 8 days of further decay from a classic news spike makes it near-certain the Mar 11 value will be far below the 32 threshold needed for "Doesn't change"
- The only countervailing force is the ongoing Iran conflict potentially generating new Ahmadinejad-specific news, but even generous Monte Carlo scenarios for this produce 99.6% Decreases
- Small probability mass on Increases (3%) accounts for an unprecedented Ahmadinejad-specific re-spike in the active conflict
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “ahmadinejad” change between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-11 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42405)
